behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is checker logic on template text, not a module-handoff-only failure. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No OS/network/subprocess required; in-process input strings reproduce leakage. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No model call. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check" (single-check logic), with no required harness/file-loading interaction in the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint rule function with template fixtures: one clean template, one containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md; assert reject/accept decisions and leakage location reporting.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from the real harness (after interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit tests of interpolation helpers can pass while assembled prompt still leaks due to cross-module composition (template + fixture + renderer). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local wiring, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-governed handoff. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about assembled prompt content pre-execution, not live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline with production-like fixtures and interpolation; assert final emitted prompt text has no forbidden phase-instruction snippets from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): A unit test of loader/parser may pass while invocation builder still injects hard-coded rules instead of loaded context. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No mandatory real network/subprocess, local file-backed module wiring is sufficient, (b) can validate source-of-truth wiring without relying on model quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain required. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic because this behavior can be validated by inspecting invocation construction and prompt sources; text does not require live model judgments here.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real harness invocation construction against plugin skill files; assert context in the invocation originates from file-loaded sections and that prompt template does not include duplicated hard-coded phase rules.

behaviors.description: Harness runs the same user-style prompt against degraded/missing SKILL.md variant without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit tests of variant selector can pass while full harness mutates prompt text or fails to swap skill variant in end-to-end assembly. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No required real external infra beyond local file/module wiring, (b) does not require model-output correctness, (c) no chained agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on harness capability and invariant prompt text, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness twice (baseline vs degraded/missing skill variant) with identical user prompt literal; assert prompt text bytes are unchanged while loaded skill-context source differs as configured.

behaviors.description: If relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): With a stubbed/deterministic model, test can still pass or fail arbitrarily without exposing whether real model behavior regressed due to missing skill guidance. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output changing when skill content degrades. MULTI-STEP: single model decision can suffice; workflow not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Workflow but rejected because behavior text specifies one prompt comparison/failure condition, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model with unchanged user prompt against baseline skill vs degraded skill variant; enforce that degraded variant violates behavioral assertions that baseline satisfies.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit tests of parser or artifact writer alone miss ordering bugs across pipeline stages (parse invoked before persist in orchestrator). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No real external infra required, (b) no dependence on model judgment quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic because behavior is sequencing/instrumentation around output handling, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness pipeline with a representative model response payload; assert artifact write occurs with exact raw text prior to any parse/extraction call, including when parsing later fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific field placement), not only keyword checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Core failure is assertion logic weakness (keyword-only false positives), which is local validator behavior. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: none required to prove validator semantics. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior can be demonstrated with deterministic fixtures (same keyword present but wrong field path/role) without harness/runtime coupling.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with crafted outputs: keyword present but misplaced fields/roles should fail; correctly structured outputs should pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions missed by keyword-only checks.
behaviors.justification: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): A stub model can be made to always return fixture-conformant text, hiding real regressions in field placement/structure that occur in live generations. LLM-DEP: explicitly "under live model execution" and regression detection depends on real output variability/quality. MULTI-STEP: can be single-call evaluation, so Workflow not mandatory. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Workflow but behavior text does not require multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model on target prompt set; compare structural assertion outcomes against keyword-only baseline and verify at least one real regression is caught only by structural checks.
